title: Endoscopic Sinus Surgery Foundations
subtitle: Clinical decision making and safe FESS fundamentals.
description: Build a practical framework for evaluating chronic rhinosinusitis and planning endoscopic interventions with confidence.
outcomes: Identify key sinonasal landmarks in endoscopic orientation.
Stratify CRS patients for medical versus surgical pathways.
Apply complication avoidance principles in FESS planning.
instructorBio: Consultant Rhinologist focused on minimally invasive sinus surgery and resident training.

reviews.name: Aiman R.
reviews.comment: Very practical and easy to apply in clinics.

title: Allergic Rhinitis: Modern OPD Management
subtitle: Evidence-based outpatient workflows for high-volume clinics.
description: Master fast diagnosis and treatment personalization for allergic rhinitis in real-world ENT practice.
outcomes: Classify rhinitis phenotypes accurately.
Build stepwise pharmacotherapy plans.
Counsel patients on trigger control and adherence.
instructorBio: ENT educator with focus on airway inflammation and outpatient clinical pathways.

title: Epistaxis Emergency Management
subtitle: From first response to definitive control in ENT emergencies.
description: Develop rapid stabilization and escalation skills for anterior and posterior epistaxis across settings.
outcomes: Prioritize airway and hemodynamic assessment.
Select topical, packing, and procedural interventions.
Recognize when to involve interventional services.
instructorBio: Emergency ENT consultant with extensive trauma and airway experience.

reviews.name: Farhan K.
reviews.comment: Straight to the point and clinically useful.

title: Otitis Media: Child-Focused ENT Care
subtitle: Practical pediatric otology pathways for juniors.
description: Differentiate AOM, OME, and recurrent disease while improving parent communication and follow-up.
outcomes: Distinguish common pediatric ear pathologies.
Decide observation versus antibiotics appropriately.
Plan hearing follow-up and referral timing.

curriculum.title: Diagnostic Pearls in Pediatric Ear Exams

instructorBio: Pediatric otology specialist and mentor for early-career clinicians.

reviews.name: Rimsha A.
reviews.comment: Very clear for exam and clinical work.

title: Hearing Loss Basics for ENT Trainees
subtitle: Audiology interpretation you can use in clinic tomorrow.
description: Interpret audiograms and tympanometry systematically to improve diagnosis and referral decisions.
outcomes: Read audiograms with confidence.
Differentiate conductive and sensorineural patterns.
Counsel on hearing rehab options.
instructorBio: Audiovestibular physician focused on translational teaching for residents.

title: Vertigo Workup for ENT Residents
subtitle: Fast bedside differentiation of peripheral and central causes.
description: Build diagnostic confidence in dizzy patients through structured history, exam, and red-flag screening.
outcomes: Use HINTS and bedside tests appropriately.
Differentiate BPPV, vestibular neuritis, and central vertigo.
Create safe referral and imaging pathways.
requirements: Clinical rotations in emergency or ENT
instructorBio: Neurotology lead with special interest in vestibular diagnostics.

reviews.name: Hafsa K.
reviews.comment: Best approach to dizzy patients I have seen.

title: Voice Disorders: Laryngology Intro
subtitle: Structured evaluation for hoarseness and professional voice users.
description: Learn a practical laryngology workflow from symptom analysis to laryngoscopic interpretation.
outcomes: Assess common voice complaints systematically.
Interpret core laryngoscopic findings.
Coordinate medical, behavioral, and surgical options.
instructorBio: Laryngology consultant collaborating closely with speech therapists.

reviews.name: Usman H.
reviews.comment: Good review for viva and wards.

title: Airway Assessment Basics in ENT
subtitle: First-line airway evaluation and escalation in acute settings.
description: Prepare for high-stakes ENT airway cases with robust algorithms and interdepartmental coordination.
outcomes: Perform rapid upper airway risk stratification.
Plan escalation including OR and ICU transfer triggers.
Communicate effectively in multidisciplinary emergencies.
instructorBio: ENT airway lead with trauma-center teaching background.

title: Tracheostomy Care and Complications
subtitle: Safe ward and emergency tracheostomy management for residents.
description: Develop confidence in daily tracheostomy care, troubleshooting, and emergency response.
outcomes: Standardize bedside tracheostomy assessment.
Recognize early warning signs of major complications.
Execute emergency decannulation response protocols.
instructorBio: Head and neck surgeon with strong focus on perioperative airway safety.

title: Sleep Apnea ENT Evaluation
subtitle: Clinical pathways from snoring clinic to treatment planning.
description: Understand airway collapse patterns and personalize ENT-centric management plans for sleep-disordered breathing.
outcomes: Perform focused sleep-related ENT assessments.
Interpret sleep study data in clinical context.
Compare CPAP, surgery, and adjunctive approaches.
instructorBio: ENT sleep specialist with multidisciplinary sleep-lab practice.

reviews.name: Waqas N.
reviews.comment: Helped me understand ENT role in OSA clearly.

title: Thyroid and Neck Lump Approach
subtitle: Structured ENT evaluation from first visit to referral decisions.
description: Use a stepwise, risk-based approach for thyroid and neck masses in outpatient ENT practice.
outcomes: Apply red-flag criteria in neck lump workup.
Plan imaging and FNAC pathways appropriately.
Coordinate referrals and counseling efficiently.
instructorBio: Head and neck surgeon with academic focus on early diagnosis pathways.

title: ENT OSCE & Viva Masterclass
subtitle: Exam-focused high-yield review for final-year medical students.
description: Comprehensive prep for common ENT stations, viva questions, and rapid-fire clinical reasoning.
outcomes: Perform strongly in common ENT OSCE stations.
Answer high-yield viva questions confidently.
Use structured communication under exam pressure.
instructorBio: Academic ENT tutor and examination coach for undergraduate learners.
